summary: Book's biggest risk is distribution, not a single blow-up: the desk is 9/4 over the weekly buy cap while 3 of 5 holdings carry live dilution/distribution overhangs (UUUU $300M ATM + escalating CEO Form 144 selling, NTSK post-lockup VC distribution, WRAP $200M baby-shelf) -- EDGAR shows NO new offering drew today, all 5 positions reconcile to the broker, and every scan candidate above the pack (AVO/JEM/PRQR/TDIC) is a verified dilution/pump/accumulation trap already vetoed.

WRAP.note: $200M universal baby-shelf standing (last S-3 activity Feb 2026) on a sub-$100M post-+48%-pump name = drip-dilution capacity; EDGAR shows NO July 424B5 draw. 7/13 8-K is a $2M cash outlay for Frenel preferred (an investment, NOT a raise) -- modestly raises odds of a future shelf tap to refill cash. Position +$3.82 at $2.20. FinBERT -0.853 'unusual volume' item is noise.

UUUU.note: Active $300M ATM (2026-06-13) undrawn per EDGAR (7/13 8-K is a strategy 8-K, not an offering), but CEO Chalmers insider SELLING is now larger than last snapshot: Form 144s for 200k executed 7/2 ($2.9M), 200k 6/29, +300k listed 7/9 -- distribution outweighs the smaller Bhappu/Hansen buys behind the score. Lorentzian k-NN bearish. Position -$1.37 at $13.24.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution/shelf). Nightly audit's broker_disagrees drift (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3%) is stale from 08:18 and has converged intraday -- broker now $16.92, shares reconcile 'match'. Position -$0.89.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$6.18 at $14.28, breakout catalyst intact, but post-IPO-lockup distribution persists: Lightspeed XII Class-B conversions/sales (7/9, ~230k) atop the 7/8 Form 144. ICONIQ's 610k open-market buy (7/10) partly offsets, but the large float unlock is the standing risk masked by the pop. Hiring pulse healthy (153 openings, no flag).

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback. Position +$0.56 at $5.45. Hiring pulse 24 openings, no flag. No fresh negative catalyst.

flags.why: Top non-held scan name (5.2, below line) with a 12-for-1 reverse split effective 7/14 (new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 compliance, on an unfunded 'AI+blockchain+crypto/stablecoin' pivot pump. Reverse-split-plus-crypto-pump, not a catalyst. FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline.

Scan candidate (5.17) that diluted ~30%: $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement priced 6/25/closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.72 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's 7/7 buy came AFTER the raise.

Scan candidate (5.1) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.60, no fundamental driver. Reject on the pump veto; nightly audit also flags cache stale.

Scan candidate (2.96, below line) with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M sh out, active $300M S-3 shelf (7/8), 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved.

Distressed reverse-merger pop: definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still on today's scan (2.06).

On the daily watchlist but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, earliest close ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk.

Watchlist name that priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; ~$10 sits above mean analyst PT. Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On the research avoid list.
Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. Desk avoid list; not held.

Distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround.

dilution_watch.note: $300M ATM usable now, EDGAR shows NO July draw (7/13 8-K is a strategy 8-K). Insider selling escalating: Chalmers Form 144s -- 200k executed 7/2 ($2.9M), 200k 6/29, +300k listed 7/9.
$200M universal baby-shelf for a sub-$100M post-pump name; EDGAR shows NO July 424B5 draw. 7/13 8-K is a $2M Frenel investment (cash outflow), not a raise -- standing dilution capacity only.
$50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement; closed 6/26. 7/6 F-3 registers 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. No NEW July offering.
~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition; existing holders diluted to ~53% of combined co; DOJ second request pushes earliest close to ~9/29.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants; authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: Overtrading (carried): radar 'weekly buy budget 9/4 used' -- the book is well over the 4-buy weekly cap. Surface as a standalone discipline flag independent of any single ticker.

UUUU: intel carries the name 'no boost' but still frames the Bhappu/Hansen insider BUYS prominently; EDGAR shows CEO Chalmers now has THREE Form 144 sale notices (200k executed 7/2 for $2.9M, 200k 6/29, 300k listed 7/9) that outweigh those buys -- the insider signal behind the score is net-negative, reinforcing my elevated grade.

RIG: intel's 7/14 alert concurs on the Valaris all-stock deal + DOJ second request and tags it 'do-not-chase', but still carries RIG on the active watchlist; I keep it an elevated DO-NOT-BUY given ~53% dilution and a binary vote/antitrust event.

AVO (Mission Produce): top non-held scan candidate (5.24) on a +5.0 insider score, but EDGAR shows that signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack takeover-style accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan -- not management conviction. No S-3/ATM (only 2026 dilution was the completed Calavo S-4/424B3, offset by a $100M buyback). Not a dilution/pump veto and below the buy line, so no flag -- but the score's insider component is mis-attributed.
